title: Sonnet To Byron.
content:
[I am afraid these verses will not please you, but]
    If I esteemed you less, Envy would kill
    Pleasure, and leave to Wonder and Despair
    The ministration of the thoughts that fill
    The mind which, like a worm whose life may share
    A portion of the unapproachable,
    Marks your creations rise as fast and fair
    As perfect worlds at the Creator's will.

    But such is my regard that nor your power
    To soar above the heights where others [climb],
    Nor fame, that shadow of the unborn hour
    Cast from the envious future on the time,
    Move one regret for his unhonoured name
    Who dares these words: - the worm beneath the sod
    May lift itself in homage of the God.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "Sonnet To Byron.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
